notes: The satusehat adaptor exposes only generic HTTP verbs (get, post, put, patch) against the SatuSehat Platform FHIR R4 REST API; it has no resource-specific operations. This spec was hand-authored from the SatuSehat developer docs (satusehat.kemkes.go.id) and FHIR R4 conventions. It models a representative subset of FHIR resources the adaptor targets (Patient, Encounter, Observation, Condition, Organization, Location) with read/search/create/update ops under the /fhir-r4/v1 base path, plus the OAuth2 client_credentials token endpoint (/oauth2/v1/accesstoken). Base URLs: production https://api-satusehat.kemkes.go.id, sandbox https://api-satusehat-stg.dto.kemkes.go.id. FHIR resource schemas are simplified subsets, not the full R4 StructureDefinitions or SatuSehat FHIR IG profiles. Search endpoints return FHIR Bundle (searchset); errors return OperationOutcome. SatuSehat supports many more FHIR resources (Practitioner, Procedure, Medication, MedicationRequest, Composition, etc.) not modeled here.
